what_it_does: Summarizes research evidence for the deep-rebuild loop, organized into rounds covering existing foundation state, the index.html monolith, implicit-reference inventory, content/presentation structure, and outward surfaces.
observations: States that this synthesis is an evidence base for COMPLETION_CRITERIA.md and IMPLEMENTATION_GUIDE.md.
Records foundation properties including dna/ faces, engine derivation/checking, types.json registries, rebuild.sh proofs, and FORM design substrate files.
Describes index.html as a large monolith with CSS and JS line ranges, named JS clusters, shared mutable state, and a decomposition pattern using DNA.rooms.register.
Classifies implicit references into three tiers: declare now, formalize fields then declare, and leave model-checked.
Identifies specimen-tree.json as the target content shape and grown.json as a pointer tree rather than HTML.
States a scoped gap: add content home, registry, binding field, and one renderer without migrating all 73 pieces.
Notes outward surfaces including the in-repo design substrate, Claude Code session loops plus cron continuation, and visual-factory consuming dist/.
standouts: The file ties each round to implementation implications rather than only summarizing findings.
It distinguishes verified file-altitude claims from tracer-sweep claims that need later verification.
It frames content as an authored-half artifact outside dna/ while still participating in declared registries.
conventions: Uses round-based sections with compact evidence bullets.
Uses emphatic uppercase labels to mark important claims and implications.
Combines measurements, line ranges, file paths, and procedural conclusions in the same synthesis document.
Marks verification status directly in headings or bullets.
concerns: Several bullets compress evidence, interpretation, and next-step guidance into one line, which can make individual claim boundaries hard to isolate.
The file depends on many named external artifacts and entries, so some claims cannot be checked from this file alone.
Embedded line counts and line ranges may become stale if the referenced files change.
notes: Brief this as the loop’s research ledger: it records what the repo already shows, what references can be formalized, and what scoped plumbing the rebuild should add.
questions: What are the six proofs named by rebuild.sh?
Where is the wave A source report for the index.html cluster ranges?
Which completion criteria consume each round of this synthesis?
What exact field shape is expected for application.navigation verbs?
What small set of pieces should prove the first address-resolving renderer?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the actual content: it functions as an evidence synthesis for downstream completion criteria and implementation guidance.
apparent_themes: Separation of authored content from identity DNA
Small proven plumbing before broad migration
Continuity through rebuild proofs, commits, and loop records
intention_signals: It seems intended to preserve the factual research substrate for a rebuild loop so future implementation can proceed from recorded repo evidence, scoped gaps, and declared verification caveats.
novelty: It treats design, type declarations, content structure, build proofs, and loop continuity as connected substrates in one research ledger.
contribution: Contributes a neutral synthesis of observed repo structures, declared registries, decomposition opportunities, implicit-reference tiers, content-shape findings, scoped renderer/registry needs, and continuation constraints that a fused system would retain as evidence and orientation.
summary_for_embedding: Research synthesis for counterpart-design’s deep-rebuild loop, documenting foundation state, index.html decomposition findings, implicit-reference tiers, content/presentation architecture, scoped gaps, and outward-surface constraints.
intention_for_embedding: Created to ground completion criteria and implementation guidance in repo-observed evidence while marking verification caveats and scoped next work.
